title: ERC7821 minimal batch executor with mode dispatch, optional opData, and batch-of-batches recursion
explanation: Three layers: (1) Mode dispatch packs the EIP-7579 mode bytes [0..1] (0x0100) and [6..9] (0x00000000 / 0x78210001 / 0x78210002) into a uint id of 1, 2, or 3 via three eq() checks combined with OR shifts. id=0 means invalid mode → revert. (2) Optional opData detection: if id==2 and the executionData offset leaves room for a second offset (the head word > 0x40), opData is decoded from offsets 0x20 onwards. The trick `gt(eq(id, 2), gt(0x40, head))` is true only when both id==2 AND head > 0x40 — branchless filter. (3) Batch-of-batches (id=3) recursively calls execute() with mode XOR'd to flip the trailing 0002 → 0001, so each inner batch is processed as a regular opData-supporting batch. Each recursive call gets its own opData scope, useful for separately-signed sub-batches in EIP-7702 use cases. (4) Per-call execution uses the standard call + bubble revert pattern; results are intentionally NOT collected, keeping the executor mode minimal.
when_to_apply: EIP-7702 batchers, smart accounts implementing the ERC7821 minimal-executor interface, batch-of-batches multi-signer flows
when_not_to_apply: Accounts requiring per-call result collection (use ERC4337 executeBatch instead), strict-mode executors that must reject unknown modes more loudly
